examples: Time and again, Mr. Heaney returns to the image of the pen. He began his long career writing of such a pen, nestling snug as a gun between finger and thumb.
A classic example is noisy begging by nestling songbirds when a parent returns to the nest with food.
Therefore, a begging nestling that secures food at the expense of its siblings might actually leave behind fewer copies of its genes overall than it might otherwise.
He powerfully struck a first-time effort and saw it take a major deflection before nestling in the corner of the net.
Celebrate the tastes of the season by taking seared mackerel fillets and nestling them into a garlic-infused mound of English peas.
